summary: Services for selected festivals of the ecclesiastical year
coll: II ff. 1–7 (incomplete) ; III ff. 7–14 ; IV ff. 15–22 ; V ff. 23–30 ; VI ff. 31–38 ; VII ff. 39–47 (f. 44 is a singleton) ; VIII ff. 48–55 ; IX ff. 56–63 ; X ff. 64–71 ; XI ff. 72–79 ; XII ff. 80–87 ; XIII ff. 88–95 ; XIV ff. 96–103 ; XV ff. 104–110 (incomplete)

note: Black and red ink used in the text

work.desc_title: Services for selected festivals of the ecclesiastical year

excerpt.as_written: ܥܠ ܚܝܠܐ ܕܡܪܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܡܫܝܚܐ ܟܬܒܝܢܢ ܠܡܪܝ ܡܘܫܐ ܕ ܒܐܝܠܘܠ
.ܐܘ ܪܝܫܟܗܢܐ ܟܝܪܝܠܠܘܣ. ܫܐܠ ܠܢ ܖ̈ܚܡܐ ܘܚܘܣܝ ܚܘܒܐ

contents.label: 4 Ēlūl (September), St Moses [the Prophet]
note: Canon to the Holy Trinity, mode 4 (ff. 15r–19v): Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9
Another canon, “composition by the bishop Metrophanes”, mode 2 (ff. 19v–25r): Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9

contents.label: Thursday of the fifth week of the Lent
note: Stichera of the Great Canon on Repentance, mode 6 (ff. 26v–29r)
Makarismoi of the Great Canon on Repentance “that we sing after ode 6”, mode 6 (ff. 29r–32r)

Canon, “composition by the venerable Andrew, the bishop of the island of Crete, translated from Greek into Syriac by John bar ‘Īsā, the priest in the city of Rome” (ff. 32v–82v): Ode 1 (ff. 32v–37r); Ode 2 (ff. 37r–40r); Ode 2, “another heirmos” (ff. 40r–42v); Ode 3 (ff. 42v–46r); Ode 3, “another heirmos” (ff. 46r–47r); Ode 4 (ff. 47r–54r); Ode 4, “from the triode by father Joseph, mode 8” (f. 54r–v); Ode 4, “by father Theodore” (ff. 54v–55r); Ode 5 (ff. 55r–59v); Ode 6 (ff. 59v–62v); Ode 7 (ff. 62v–67r); Ode 8 (ff. 67r–72v); Ode 8, “heirmos from the triode, mode 8” (ff. 72v–73r); [Ode 8, heirmos] (f. 73r–v); Ode 9 (ff. 73v–81r); [Ode 9], “from the triode, mode 8” (f. 81r–v); [Ode 9], heirmos (ff. 81v–82v)
“Stichera after Glory to God in the highest of the canon on repentance”, mode 4 (ff. 83r–91r)

contents.label: 2 Ēlūl (September), Holy Martyr Mamas of Caesarea
note: Troparion on God is the Lord, mode 4 (f. 91r)
Canon, “composition by father John”, mode 4 (ff. 93r–101v): Odes 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9

program.label: NEH Syriac Parchment Descriptions Grant
program.description: Catalogued as part of UCLA Library's "Identification and Description of the Syriac and Arabic Manuscripts at St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, Phase 1: Syriac Parchment Manuscripts" project (2022-2025), funded by the National Endowment for the Humanities. Principle cataloguers were Grigory Kessel (Lead cataloguer), Natalia Smelova (Associate cataloguer), and Vevian Zaki (Cataloguer for Arabic content in Syriac manuscripts).

program.label: NEH Syriac Parchment Descriptions Grant
program.description: Catalogued as part of UCLA Library's "Identification and Description of the Syriac and Arabic Manuscripts at St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, Phase 1: Syriac Parchment Manuscripts" project (2022-2025), funded by the National Endowment for the Humanities. Principle cataloguers were Grigory Kessel (Lead cataloguer), Natalia Smelova (Associate cataloguer), and Vevian Zaki (Cataloguer for Arabic content in Syriac manuscripts).

program.label: Sinai Library Digitization Project, Phase 1
program.description: Digitized under the Sinai Library Digitization Project, Phase 1 (2018-2023), a collaboration of St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, with funding from the Arcadia Fund, the Ahmanson Foundation, and the Steinmetz Family Foundation. The project provided high-resolution images and basic description of the Arabic and Syriac manuscripts held in the Monastery’s library.

image_provenance.rights: All manuscript images are the property of St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai. No part of these images may be reproduced, reused, or distributed without prior written permission. For permissions and reuse requests, please contact sinai@library.ucla.edu.
